Welcome to Mafia Game 241

TimeTouristTM Mafia




Story
Notice Board

Important Alert! Do not post unless you have applied for and have been accepted as a TimeTouristTM for the 912th WormholeTM by the UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM department. If you meet the qualifications, proceed.

Dear TimeTouristsTM,

Thank you for using our TimeTravelTM service provided by the UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM department. Due to a recent surge in complaints, the CustomerServiceTM department has been working overtime to answer customer dissatisfaction emails. However, we have found that most of these complaints are caused by SheerStupidityTM on the part of the client and that some of these cannot be fixed or remedied in any way by our UnpaidInternsTM. Therefore, to make your one-year trip safer and more pleasant, please read the following general guidelines for TimeTravelTM and sign below:
  1. Accidents can occur during FourthDimensionalTM travel, including but not limited to radiation poisoning, mutilation, and death. To avoid such accidents, please fasten your seatbelts, wear your kevlar vests and lead jackets, and do not move when inside the WormholeTM. UnpaidInternsCentralTM is not liable for any damages, deaths, or disappearances of people, pets, or property that may occur during FourthDimensionalTM travel.
  2. It is illegal (UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OCRSTM Rule 9238210.3819.128B) to expose your identity as a time traveler to anyone, directly or indirectly. Specifically, the 1.5 million citizens of AncientEgyptTM need not be informed that you wrote your post-doctorate thesis on the construction of the GreatPyramidOfGizaTM. Failure to comply with this rule may result in the immediate termination of your trip, a fine of up to ten thousand billion dollars, and a lifetime in prison. You will also be responsible for the costs of fixing the mess you created which include but are not limited to the costs of operating the MemoryWiper2000TM 1.5 million times for each individual citizen, as well as the costs for the large amount of WormholesTM that would be required for transport from and to AncientEgyptTM for this procedure.
  3. Creating or attempting to create any time paradoxes is illegal (UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OCRSTM Rule 2183882.212134.32A). On one hand, attempting to kill your own grandfather is just a downright immoral thing to do, while on the other, your trip will be immediately terminated, you will receive a fine of up to fifteen thousand billion dollars, and you will be sentenced to a lifetime in prison. Trying to have a CircularFamilyTreeTM consisting of only yourself is beyond disgusting and falls under the same category (mathjayTM’s opinion on this matter has been overruled).
  4. It is very highly not recommended, though not against the rules, to inform someone of future events or attempt to change the past as long as you neither expose your identity as a TimeTouristTM nor create any TimeParadoxesTM along the way. Please note that if you tell your friend exactly how and when he will die, you will most likely push him into another TimelineTM in which he does not die in said manner, making you look like an AbsoluteFoolTM. Even on the highly rare occasion that your friend is actually stupid enough to die in said manner even after being told how and when he/she/it/they will die, it is not like you will be able to say "I told you so" anyway.
  5. All laws in ModernDayTM apply to you at any point in time. Committing a ModernDayTM crime in the past or future will result in an immediate termination of your trip along with the consequences for violating said law. You will also be bound to obey the laws of your destination, along with any punishments or other consequences that come with violating them. We will be unable to help you if you are burnt at the stake after being accused of witchcraft for making a phone call in Salem, Massachusetts.
  6. On the not-so-rare occasion that you are treated as a god or deity of any form in any civilization at any point in time, UnpaidInternCentralTM will receive 50% of any tributes or offerings that you have been given upon your return. This is in accordance with UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OCRSTM Rule 341878493.432.222J.
  7. It would be inadvisable to pay for food in AncientRomeTM with USDollarsTM, so we provide a CurrencyExchangeTM service not for your convenience but for our profits. Our CurrencyExchangeTM converts to and from over two billion different currencies for only a SmallFeeTM of 50% of the converted sum. We are also not liable for any counterfeit coins or bills that you may have received during the exchange process, though you will be prosecuted for trying to give us counterfeit money (UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OCRSTM Rule 994874827.32423.4482T).
  8. You must return to the location of your arrival exactly one year from the time you arrive. Lateness is not tolerated. We will not be responsible if you miss the return trip and end up stuck in AncientGreeceTM forever, or until the next batch of travelers arrive, whichever comes first.
  9. It is illegal (UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OCRSTM Rule 12983917.435342.333M) to attempt to bring any artifacts or people from the past or future back to the present unless you have an approved application which has been signed by one of our UnpaidInternsTM. Violation of this will result in a fine of up to fifty million dollars. To obtain an application, please call the CustomerServiceTM department.
  10. In the unlikely event of any catastrophic disasters that you may or may not have caused with your PeaSizedBrainsTM, you may arrive in person at the headquarters of UnpaidInternCentralTM, located at the center of the SupermassiveBlackHoleTM in the middle of the MilkyWayGalaxyTM. Good luck finding it.
  11. Though this sheet contains the most important guidelines for TimeTravelTM, understand that you are still liable to be prosecuted for infracting any of the googolplex rules in the UnpaidInternCentralTM TimeTravelTM OverComplicatedRuleSetTM signed upon registration, which include but are not limited to the important guidelines listed on this sheet. It is recommended that you review the full OCRSTM before the trip. Though it may take an estimated six trillion years, it will greatly improve the quality of your vacation and help you avoid most unpleasant surprises that may occur. However, we must warn you that, for causes still unknown to the UnpaidInternsTM, there seems to be a strong positive correlation between reading the full OCRSTM and missing the time of departure (which is exactly three days from now) by an average of 5.99999999999 trillion years.

I understand the above statements and consent to the terms of TimeTravelTM.
Signed,
X____________________


Please sign this contract and return it to UnpaidInternCentralTM by no later than September 7th. Your trip to 912 A.D. via the 912th WormholeTM will depart on September 12th at exactly 9:12 AM. In case you’re wondering what a WormholeTM looks like, here it is:




Thank you and we hope you have a wonderful journey.

This contract was reviewed and approved by the UnpaidInternCentralTM BoardOfDirectorsTM, among those approving are BossTM SocooboTM and UnpaidInternsTM mathjayTM, HannahWTM, MathletesvTM, and Scipio1TM, along with other unimportant and thus unnamed UnpaidInternsTM. If you have any questions or concerns, please call UnpaidInternCentralTM at NaN-NaN-NaNTM, where you will find a MonotoneRoboticVoiceTM who misinterprets anything that you say until you give up, or email us at nonexistentemail@unpaidintern.orgTM, where all your emails will go directly into the SpamFolderTM and will never be heard from again.


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 12 days while each "night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on a modified 09:12 setup. Please refer to the setup tab for details.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by mathjay and Socoobo.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (단건우형 ㅠㅠ#5081/s0c00b0#5989).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.
  • Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! Be sure to post a 4-digit number of your choice in the thread!

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • mathjay and Socoobo are the moderators of Mafia Game 241.
    • ad333, fdas, HannahW, Mathletesv, and Scipio1 have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG241OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 9.
    Current version: 9.1
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on a modified version of the 09:12 setup, which is a 9p semi-open setup. All setup mechanics remain unchanged with the exception of the list of roles, which was changed to the following:
  1. Town Tracker
  2. 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie
  3. Mafia Rolecop
  4. Town Follower
  5. Town Jailkeeper
  6. Mafia 1-Shot Strongman
  7. Town Babysitter
  8. Town Roleblocker
  9. Mafia Rolecop
  10. Town Rolecop
  11. Town Doctor
  12. Mafia Roleblocker
3 consecutive PRs (ie., 4, 5, 6 or 11, 12, 1) will be randomly chosen from the list, and 5 VTs and 1 Mafia Goon will be added to complete a playerlist of 9.

There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Both VTs and the Mafia Goon will show up as "Vanilla" to the rolecops.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • If you have any questions about action resolution priority, please PM the mods.

Significant Events

Dawn has ended and Day 1 has started.
ad333 replaces violetmx12.

Day 1 has ended and Night 1 has started.
Night 1 has ended and Day 2 has started.

alpha_zom replaces Made_in_2004.
Night 2 has ended and Day 3 has started.
Day 3 has ended and Night 3 has started.
Night 3 has ended and Day 4 has started. It is now LyLo.
Game has ended. Congratulations to the mafia for winning.

[*]Though this sheet contains the most important guidelines for TimeTravel[sup]TM[/sup], understand that you are still liable to be prosecuted for infracting any of the googolplex rules in the UnpaidInternCentral[sup]TM[/sup] TimeTravel[sup]TM[/sup] OverComplicatedRuleSet[sup]TM[/sup] signed upon registration, which include but are not limited to the important guidelines listed on this sheet. It is recommended that you review the full OCRS[sup]TM[/sup] before the trip. Though it may take an estimated six trillion years, it will greatly improve the quality of your vacation and help you avoid most unpleasant surprises that may occur. However, we must warn you that, for causes still unknown to the UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup], there seems to be a strong positive correlation between reading the full OCRS[sup]TM[/sup] and missing the time of departure (which is exactly three days from now) by an average of 5.99999999999 trillion years.[/list]

I understand the above statements and consent to the terms of TimeTravel[sup]TM[/sup].

[right]Signed,[/right]
[right]X____________________[/right]


Please sign this contract and return it to UnpaidInternCentral[sup]TM[/sup] by no later than September 7th. Your trip to 912 A.D. via the 912th Wormhole[sup]TM[/sup] will depart on September 12th at exactly 9:12 AM. In case you’re wondering what a Wormhole[sup]TM[/sup] looks like, here it is:

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/886565454863274004/worm_apple_resize.png[/img]


Thank you and we hope you have a wonderful journey.

If you have any questions or concerns, please call UnpaidInternCentral[sup]TM[/sup] at NaN-NaN-NaN[sup]TM[/sup], where you will find a MonotoneRoboticVoice[sup]TM[/sup] who misinterprets anything that you say until you give up, or email us at nonexistentemail@unpaidintern.org[sup]TM[/sup], where all your emails will go directly into the SpamFolder[sup]TM[/sup] and will never be heard from again.[/size]

[hide=- Important Rules -]
[b]This tab summarizes the most important rules.[/b]

[b]For experienced players:[/b] this is the only tab you need to read. 
[b]For new players:[/b] if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful. 

[b]Integrity[/b]
[list][*][b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*][b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request. 
[*][b]Do not gamethrow[/b] or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*][b]Do not attach files[/b] or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*][b]Do not use OGI[/b] (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games. [/list]

[b]Activity[/b]
[list][*]Each [b]"day" is 12 days[/b] while each [b]"night" is 2 days[/b].
[*][b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*]This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules. 
[*]All [b]times[/b] will be in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC-5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable. 
[*]Activity rules are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b][/list]

[b]Voting[/b]
[list][*]You [b]must format your votes correctly.[/b] Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated. 
[*][b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2.
[*][b]Deadline hang is on[/b] from Day 1 but it is [b]off[/b] for LYLO. 
[*]Votes are [b]locked at LYLO.[/b][/list]

[b]Etiquette and Warnings[/b]

[list][*][b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text [b]hard to read.[/b]
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but [b]do not make personal attacks[/b] on players themselves. 
[*]First-timers can receive up to [b]3 warnings[/b] before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. [b]All other players can receive up to 2. [/b]
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.[/list] 

[b]Setup and Moderators[/b]

[list][*]This game is based on a [b]modified 09:12 setup.[/b] Please refer to the setup tab for details. 
[*]Mafia have access to [b]daychat[/b]. 
[*]All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are [b]standard. [/b]
[*]This game is moderated by mathjay and Socoobo. 
[*]This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.[/list]

[b]Resources and Support[/b]

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (단건우형 ㅠㅠ#5081/s0c00b0#5989). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below. 
[*]For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517106_important_resources]here[/url].
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM. 
[*]Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! Be sure to post a 4-digit number of your choice in the thread! [/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p23135586]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p23135588]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p23135593]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]mathjay and Socoobo are the moderators of Mafia Game 241. 
[*]ad333, fdas, HannahW, Mathletesv, and Scipio1 have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.

[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

post_rendered:
To all UnpaidInternsTM in the TimeTravelTM department,

I hope y’all are having a fantastic day because I’m about to ruin it.

I have BadNewsTM. It’s VERY, VERY, VERY BadNewsTM. It’s even worse than the time I stubbed my toe on mathjayTM’s face. And by the way, I’ll have you know I only cried about that for 20 minutes.

Three days ago on September 12th at 9:12 A.M., ships travelling in the 912th WormholeTM whose destination was 912 A.D. just completely disappeared. They just went BippityBoppityPOOFTM and vanished.

We suspect that TimeTerroristsTM from PaidInternCentralTM hacked it with their MediocreHackingSkillsTM. Remind MeTM to fire the NetworkSecurityGuysTM tomorrow. Anyways, they’ve all been brought to the TopSecretTM location, and we’ve detected three more unknown ships heading that way as well. But that’s not even the worst part. I could care less about the TimeTouristsTM. The worst part is that THEY’RE HEADED TOWARDS mathjayTM WHO IS ON A TopSecretTM MISSION!!!!!!!

TimeTerroristsTM are cruel, merciless, and absolutely terrifying. They will kill your brain cells faster than you can recite the Gettysburg Address in Arabic. Here is a picture of a TimeTerroristTM:




We must begin searching for the lost ship immediately before the media gets word of this. We will all look like AbsoluteFoolsTM if the public finds out. If you don’t find anything by yesterday, YOU’RE ALL FIRED!

But anyway, that doesn’t exempt you from BACK-BREAKING, SKULL-PIERCING, CRUEL AND UNUSUAL, UnpaidTM LABOR! Here’s the to-do list for today. As usual, if you don’t get through all twenty tasks today, YOU’RE ALL FIRED!
  1. Look at see-through glass and when someone is on the other side shout "OH MY GOD, I'M HIDEOUS!"
  2. Bring a big chair into the elevator facing away from the door and when someone walks in, dramatically turn and say, “We have been expecting you.”
  3. Walk up to someone, hand them a potato, look them in the eyes and warn them, “With great power, comes great responsibility.” Then walk away in a nonchalant manner.
  4. Call someone and tell them you can't talk right now.
  5. Point at someone and shout, "You're one of them!" Run and pretend to trip. Crawl away slowly.
  6. Buy a donut and complain that there's a hole in it.
  7. Put mayonnaise in a bowl, freeze it, and tell your friend it's ice cream.
  8. Put up a "Lost Dog" poster with a picture of a cat on it.
  9. In a public toilet, pass a note under the door next to you saying, "They're onto us. We need to go."
  10. Walk up to a random person and say, "Wow! You've changed, I still have your picture from five years ago." And hold up a picture of a potato.
  11. Call McDonalds asking for directions to Burger King.
  12. Order a pizza 5 minutes before New Year’s Day, and when it comes, yell, "I ORDERED THIS THING A YEAR AGO!”
  13. Bring a desk on an elevator. When people try to get on, ask if they have an appointment.
  14. Go to an electronic store with a banana and say that you want to upgrade to an apple.
  15. Call Pizza Hut and ask for the phone number to Domino's.
  16. Hide a walkie-talkie in a bush and scare people that walk by.
  17. Get on a crowded elevator with a bag in your hands, sigh and say, "Darn, my snake got loose again."
  18. When someone asks you if you know what time it is, say yes and walk away.
  19. Dress up as a duck and throw bread at people and yell, "HOW DO YOU LIKE IT?!! HUH?!?!?”
  20. Go to a pet shop, point at an employee, and scream, "I WANT THAT ONE MOMMY!!!"

Thank you and I hope you have a terrible rest of your day.

To all UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup] in the TimeTravel[sup]TM[/sup] department,

I hope y’all are having a fantastic day because I’m about to ruin it.

I have BadNews[sup]TM[/sup]. It’s VERY, VERY, VERY BadNews[sup]TM[/sup]. It’s even worse than the time I stubbed my toe on mathjay[sup]TM[/sup]’s face. And by the way, I’ll have you know I only cried about that for 20 minutes.

Three days ago on September 12th at 9:12 A.M., ships travelling in the 912th Wormhole[sup]TM[/sup] whose destination was 912 A.D. just completely disappeared. They just went BippityBoppityPOOF[sup]TM[/sup] and vanished.

We suspect that TimeTerrorists[sup]TM[/sup] from PaidInternCentral[sup]TM[/sup] hacked it with their MediocreHackingSkills[sup]TM[/sup]. Anyways, they’ve all been brought to the TopSecret[sup]TM[/sup] location, and we’ve detected three more unknown ships heading that way as well. But that’s not even the worst part. Thank you and I hope you have a terrible rest of your day.

Attention all UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup],

I have GoodNews[sup]TM[/sup], your jobs are saved.

The location of the ships has been ascertained by one of our most talented UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup] Scipio1[sup]TM[/sup]. You all should be looking up to him as a role model. He has now been EmployeeOfTheMonth[sup]TM[/sup] for 1348090189041 consecutive months by virtue of him winning literally every single ReaperGame[sup]TM[/sup] by a margin of over 200,000. For the rest of you: I’m not mad, I’m just Disappointed[sup]TM[/sup].

You are now tasked with writing a speech that I will present at the next media conference to CoverUp[sup]TM[/sup] this UnpaidDisaster[sup]TM[/sup]. IF YOU DON’T GET IT DONE BY TWO MONTHS AGO, YOU’RE ALL FIRED[sup]TM[/sup]!

What’s that? How do we safely get the TimeTourists[sup]TM[/sup] back, you ask? We don’t! Screw them, they’re all idiots who decided to spend money on a TimeTravel[sup]TM[/sup] system with absolutely no SafetyPrecautions[sup]TM[/sup] whatsoever! It’s their own fault for getting into that mess. Just remember our unofficial motto, “Customers can go ”

And here are the new tasks for the day. As always, IF YOU DON’T GET IT ALL DONE, YOU’RE ALL FIRED[sup]TM[/sup]!

[list=1]
[*]Go to a McDonalds drive through and buy a single French fry.
[*]Go to a public area and scream, “Hey, idiot!” See how many people turn around.
[*]Pretend to be a carrot.
[*]Walk up to a random stranger, hand them a spoon, and tell them, “Now, the fate of the world rests in your hands.” Look around for evesdroppers before surreptitiously walking away.
[*]Throw a book at someone’s face and yell, “YOU JUST GOT FACEBOOKED!”
[*]Call a random number and breathe into the phone loudly, then say, “I know where you live.”
[*]Go to a public area and ask random people for their opinions on the best place to hide a dead body.
[*]Write a note saying, “Sorry for the damage on your car,” and place it on a random car.
[*]The next time someone asks you why, respond, “BECAUSE I’M BATMAN!”
[*]Use duct tape to tape your neighbor to a tree.
[*]Name everything you own, and insist that everyone call your things by their proper names. For example, when someone refers to something you own as “that pencil”, scream, “HIS NAME IS RICKY!”
[*]Dress up as a Pokemon and start throwing small plastic balls at people, yelling, “IN SOVIET RUSSIA, YOU DO NOT CATCH POKEMON, POKEMON CATCH YOU!”
[*]Go into a clothing store and hide in the racks. When someone comes by, poke your head out and start screaming, “PICK ME PICK ME PICK ME!”
[*]First check if you can fit in your fridge. If not, get a bigger one (I’m not paying for it, you are). Now hide in the fridge and when someone opens it up, start singing, “The cold never bothered me anyway!”
[*]Dress up like a giant M&M, go to a public place, and start screaming, “THE SKITTLES ARE COMING!”
[/list]

Thank you, and as always, have a terrible rest of your day.

“Well done,” the OtherBoss[sup]TM[/sup] replies in a deep, raspy voice, “Now that mathjay[sup]TM[/sup] is gone, no one will ever uncover my SecretIdentity[sup]TM[/sup]!”

Just then, the PaidPhone[sup]TM[/sup] rings and OtherBoss[sup]TM[/sup] picks up.

Also, we forgot to mention that theta said that friend. made a bunch of self-preservation posts. Remember that it's always in a player's best interest to protect himself. Therefore, we don't think self-preservation posts are alignment-indicative.

In fact, we think that in some cases, self-preservation posts tend to come from town more than scum. For instance, if a town player is about to be hanged, he knows that he's 100% town, so he'll try to save himself. On the other hand, a scum player about to be hanged might actually try to speed up the hang so that he shortens town's discussion time and doesn't accidentally reveal clues about his teammates.

awesomeming327. wrote:
It is the graak elphebat

Ein Doppelkupplungsgetriebe ist ein automatisiertes Schaltgetriebe, das mittels zweier Teilgetriebe einen vollautomatischen Gangwechsel ohne Zugkraftunterbrechung ermöglicht. Die Getriebesteuerung wählt die Gänge selbsttätig oder nach Fahrerwunsch (Schaltwippen/Wählhebel) im Rahmen der zugelassenen Drehzahlbereiche. Im Gegensatz zu Automatikgetrieben mit hydraulischem Drehmomentwandler erfolgt die Übertragung des Moments über eine von zwei Kupplungen, die zwei Teilgetriebe mit dem Antrieb verbinden. Wie beim automatischen Getriebe mit hydraulischem Drehmomentwandler ermöglicht auch dieses Prinzip einen Gangwechsel ohne Zugkraftunterbrechung, indem gleichzeitig eine Kupplung schließt, während die andere öffnet.
post_canonical:
[quote=awesomeming327.]It is the graak elphebat[/quote]

Ein [b]Doppelkupplungsgetriebe[/b] ist ein automatisiertes Schaltgetriebe, das mittels zweier Teilgetriebe einen vollautomatischen Gangwechsel ohne Zugkraftunterbrechung ermöglicht. Die Getriebesteuerung wählt die Gänge selbsttätig oder nach Fahrerwunsch (Schaltwippen/Wählhebel) im Rahmen der zugelassenen Drehzahlbereiche. Im Gegensatz zu Automatikgetrieben mit hydraulischem Drehmomentwandler erfolgt die Übertragung des Moments über eine von zwei Kupplungen, die zwei Teilgetriebe mit dem Antrieb verbinden. Wie beim automatischen Getriebe mit hydraulischem Drehmomentwandler ermöglicht auch dieses Prinzip einen Gangwechsel ohne Zugkraftunterbrechung, indem gleichzeitig eine Kupplung schließt, während die andere öffnet.

To the UnpaidInternsTM working in the TimeTravelTM department,

Good morning, everyone. I hope you didn’t have an entire 8 hours of sleep last night because I certainly did! It was very restful, by the way, thank you for asking.

Because I’m so GenerousTM, the only UnpaidTaskTM is to explore the entire UnpaidInternCentralTM HQ today! There are a total of 1038129748170498710985618905610978438190841387098198401374891365891750198439143187859158137084013984013411895701938751673168848888417398430187416969696969696969696969696969696994381084917489013740318942042042042013119 floors in the building, so good luck! In fact, I’m officially calling this an UnununpaidVacationTM, because UnpaidInternCentralTM does not employ illegal labor like not paying its UnpaidInternsTM during RegularWorkHoursTM. That would be preposterous, hahahaha!

Here is a list of amenities that you had no idea existed in our HQ but totally do.

1. The most realistic MovieTheaterTM in the entire MultiverseTM. Watch scenes from your favorite movies come alive and set fire to, devour, shoot, collapse on, decapitate, flood, stomp on, cut in half, and vaporize your fellow movie-goers. It’s so realistic that you’ll actually fear for your life, but don’t worry, no one who’s ever left the theater’s ever given it a bad review. It is TotallySafeTM, so go check it out on Floor 76812376851762351786251294837123! Just watch out for the R&D lab next door, cuz they’re zapping snails with AlphabetRaysTM and some of them tend to escape from time to time.
2. A 69-star Michellin restaurant, which is on the 59827398123th floor twice removed. Everything there is complimentary, of course, and is so good that regular food will taste like DepressionTM afterwards. To get there, simply go to the 123859172351237th floor and find the dimensional transmogrifier which will remove you twice for the low, low fare of $7777777777777777777.99 each way.
3. A real indoors bungee jump, where you can experience the thrill of falling to your untimely death without actually dying! Probably. It’s located in the 5th quadrant of Floor 471291230152; just look for the elevator that says, “Danger! Out of service!” and head right in!
4. A computer lab where you can play Dota2TM or its far inferior counterpart LeagueOfLemmingsTM or something for free1 at lightning fast speed of up to 120 frames per hour. Those Windows192837918527129837123 PCs cost a fortune, but it’s worth it2, amiright or amiright? Just follow the signs on the 14th floor.
5. The UnpaidCafeTM. It’s the best coffee and tea you’ll ever taste at the shockingly affordable price of $9999.99 a cup. All their coffee is made from the best GenuineCardboardTM, imported all the way from China. Also, it’s staffed by UnpaidMiceTM because everyone knows mice are more competent than interns. They even serve everything in cute little thimbles, isn’t that adorable?
6. The DanceDanceRevolutionTM club, guaranteed to give you arthritis 70 years too early! It’s the perfect way to invest in your future by racking up quintillions of dollars in preexisting conditions and chronic illnesses. Start planning to drain your UnpaidRetirementFundTM TODAY, on Floor 3564516723744123. Your future self will thank you.
9. You might also want to try participating in the Daily UnpaidInternCentralTM Official CleaningCompetitionTM. Compete with your friends3 or other random strangers for a chance to win amazing prizes. The contestant who cleans one of UnpaidInternCentralTM’s 81261478126378568172638162578123 bathrooms the fastest gets to win their very own 1 milliliter bottle of CloroxTM !
10. Go stargazing in the AstronomyTowerTM, located in the basement! Wait, hold on a second, is that a PyramidShipTM I see? Oh gosh, it’s coming t- *static*
11. Check out the MassageParlorTM. Do you have aches and pains from all the UnpaidLaborTM we totally don’t make you do? Is your neck bent into the 11th dimension because you played too much Dota2TM in the computer lab? Then check out the MassageParlorTM on the 7283764182309th floor! They offer a variety of services, including but not limited to NeckRemovalsTM, SpineRemovalsTM, and even HeadRemovalsTM, all designed to help you get the most out of your workflow. How does a SpineRemovalTM help with increasing work efficiency? We have no idea! Just get your work done or else you’re FIREDTM!
12. The CollegeApplicationAutofill3000TM, guaranteed to get you RejectedTM from every school you apply to! Hey, there is an opportunity cost to everything. No such thing as a FreeLunchTM. (But UnpaidInternsTM do exist, apparently.)
13. Buy a snack or thirty in the InfiniteTM OverpricedTM VendingMachineTM Hallway. Discovered last year on the 565178236785123th floor by an adventurous (and lost) UnpaidInternTM, current estimates say it’ll take 8672123571923 years to drive a UnpaidInternSledTM from one end of the hallway to the other. On the way, you’ll be able to buy packaged snacks for 69 times the market price and 420 times the preservatives, but hey, button pressing is fun and you know you want to.
14. Go on an adventure in the LibraryOfDoomTM. Get lost in the sprawling hallways, lined with heavy, AncientTM books that nobody reads, and never make your way back out. OohTM and AhhTM at the mystical sights of the skeletons of the brave UnpaidInternsTM who ventured the hallways before you, or try an exciting new cardio workout, called RunningTheHeckAwayFromMonstersOhMyGodTM. If you’re lucky enough, maybe you’ll find the HolyContractTM that lets you actually get paid! Try it now, on Floor 68923749812739851982369123; just go left, then right, then right, then forwards, then right again, then make a Z turn to arrive at the entrance.
1: The monetary valuation of all the time and money you will waste on these WonderfulAndLifeImprovingTM games is not factored into this price estimation. Please note that you are responsible for any lost labor via ProcrastinationTM that may happen as a result of playing these WonderfulAndLifeImprovingTM games, which will be deducted from your nonexistent paycheck.
2: UnpaidInternCentralTM is not responsible for any lagging, AFK penalties, or MMR loss as a result of getting the BlueScreenOfDeathTM. Please contact MicrosoftTM CustomerSupportTM for any issues. Or was it MicrosoftTM CustomerServiceTM? I forgot which is which, but one of them will hack all your CreditCardsTM, while the other will put you on hold longer than SnoozingnewtTM’s nose. Eh, I’m sure you can figure that out by yourself!
3: If you have them, friends not included with purchase.

Also government inspectors are coming over for tea today. It’s an entirely friendly visit, and they’re probably just here to congratulate us on our smooth and efficient handling of the TimeTouristTM CrisisTM. It also has absolutely nothing at all to do with your sudden UnununPaidVacationTM by the way. We are absolutely not ingaging in any illegal, legally questionable, shady, child labor, exploitative, or underhanded business practices here at UnpaidInternCentralTM and this government inspection is therefore UnfoundedTM and part of PaidInternCentralTM’s evil plot, no doubt.

Scared that they’ll report you for slacking off? Not to worry, I think of absolutely everything. There is already a plan in place to slap them with a BigFatTunaTM and feed them to the sharks, which are on the 19283127895712th floor by the way, if you want to come watch. In fact, please do come and get your grubby little fingerprints all over the SharkTankButtonTM; I wouldn’t want you to feel left out of the experience!

Cheers, and remember to give each other a friendly punch in the face whenever you see each other!

Socoobo
The BossTM


EulerNTM was VanquishedTM by the PaidInternsTM. He was a DogeEnthusiastTM (Vanilla Townie).

post_rendered:
“Okay guys,” fifty51TM said. “I have an important, LifeChangingTM event that’s going to happen in a bit.”

“Wait,” said ad333TM. “Really? How do you know?”

fifty51TM shrugged. “My old Granny said so, and Granny’s always right. I’ll be just over that ridge over there. It’ll take less than 5 minutes, so don’t VanquishTM anyone or set the piano OnFireTM while I’m gone, alright?”

“Okay,” said NoSignOfThetaTM.

“I sleep,” said snoozingnewtTM.

“Wait, but we don’t have a piano . . ?” said ad333TM, confused.

“Coolio,” said fifty51TM, and walked over the ridge.

The vast NuclearWastelandTM stared back at him. It was very EmptyTM. EmptierTM than your wallet or mathjayTM’s head, in fact. There were a couple giant cliffs that rose above the horizon, and the setting sun painted everything bright red, but that was ItTM.

“Hello?” fifty51TM said. “Is anyone there?”

A MysteriousPizzaTM of UnknownOriginsTM appeared in his hands.

“Oh,” fifty51TM said. “That’s cool.”

“Thank you!” he called out. The winds just whistled, swirling up some NuclearDustTM in the distance, and he started walking back. Then he started running back, because there was smoke coming from the camp and that did not look like a VeryGoodSignTM. He staggered back over the ridge and then promptly dropped his MysteriousPizzaTM.

There was now a piano in the center of the camp, and it was OnFireTM.

“Why is there a piano here? Why is it on fire? What happened?!” he cried, clutching his head like he had a migraine.

“I DON’T KNOW!” yelled ad333TM, who was running around in circles and waving his arms up and down. “It just fell out of the sky after snoozing pressed the VanquishingButtonTM.”

“SNOOZING PRESSED THE VANQUISHINGBUTTONTM?” fifty51TM yelled back. “WHY?!”

“I DON’T KNOW, ASK HIM!” ad333TM shouted, pointing.

“WHY DID YOU PRESS THE VANQUISHINGBUTTONTM?!”

“I didn’t,” grumbled snoozingnewtTM. “I slapped the VanquishingButtonTM, get your verbs right ad333TM. Also, alpha_zomTM bad. Also, real sheets.” He indeed did appear to be in possession of a real bedsheet, which he’d cocooned himself in. He rolled over and went to sleep.

“Wait, where is alpha_zomTM?” said fifty51TM. “I don’t see him anywhere.”

“He’s under the piano,” replied ad333TM glumly. “snoozingnewtTM was wrong.”

“I told you so,” mumbled alpha_zomTM faintly, from somewhere under the OnFireTM piano, before disappearing.

alpha_zom was VanquishedTM. They were a DogeEnthusiastTM (Vanilla Townie).

“BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA,” boomed a new voice. “MONKAS OMEGALUL KEKWGIF,” it continued, pronouncing the sentence like: monka-ess oh-mega-lul kek-double-u-jif.

“?????????????????????????” cried fifty51TM, spinning around. “Who are you, and what language are you speaking!? Please, today’s been stressful enough already; I just want to have my MysteriousPizzaTM of UnknownOriginsTM, is that too much to ask?”

“OH YOU POOR, INNOCENT SOUL,” came the voice, again, and this time, something in the shape of a boy shimmered into existence right in front of fifty51TM’s face. “You sweet, sweet summer child,” cooed the boy, grabbing fifty51TM’s shoulders, eyes maniacally wide. “Pizza? In this economy? My, that’s spicy~”

“Ack!” yelled fifty51TM, scrambling to get away and falling backwards onto the ground. “Who are you? Get away from me!”

“worryHands,” said the boy, pouting. He floated away, the rest of his body materializing out of thin air as he stepped onto the ground. He glowed slightly as the sun’s glow faded, and glitched slightly at the edges. “Why must you hurt me so, fifty51TM? T^T”

“What are you saying?” fifty51TM said, looking like someone just told him snails were actually the leading researchers of sphenopalatine ganglioneuralgia. “What the heck is a ‘Tee Em Tee Carrot Tee’?!”

“Ah, plebeians,” the boy said in distaste, making a tsk-tsk sound and flicking imaginary holographic dust off of his holographic hoodie. “No sense of culture. In either case, it doesn’t matter, GVfloffsmirk. I am merely here to inform you that you will die SoonTM.” He paused. “And,” he added condescendingly, “unlike those monkaW blobs over at UnpaidInternCentralTM, SoonTM here actually means, ‘in 24 hours,’ by the way.”

The 3 surviving members - snoozingnewtTM hadn’t even bothered to wake up - just stared at the boy, unimpressed.

“But we already knew that,” said NoSignOfThetaTM.

“Not if we win,” ad333TM said defensively, crossing his arms.

The boy just smiled. “That’s cute, but I’m not just talking about your little game here. In,” he tapped his wrist, “exactly 23 hours and 59 minutes, everything will be covered in water. Everything,” he said, gesturing at the mountains and plains and the NuclearWastelandTM around him. “All of this. And it’ll only be the start.” He giggled.

“Oh, and I’ll be bringing along a special guest tomorrow, bright and early, so . . .” He laughed menacingly. “Enjoy the last peaceful night you’ll ever have.”

The hologram flickered out, leaving behind only the smouldering embers of the piano and the misty swath of stars in the sky.

. . .

“YOU CALL THIS A PEACEFUL NIGHT?!”
